records.ety: [F., prob. from Prov. F. dupe, dube; of unknown origin; equiv. to F. huppe hoopoe, a foolish bird, easily caught. Cf. Armor. houprik hoopoe, a man easily deceived. Cf. also Gull, Booby.]

defs: One who has been deceived or who is easily deceived; a gull; as, the dupe of a schemer.

records.ety: [Cf. F. duper, fr. dupe. See Dupe, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Duped (dūpt); p. pr. & vb. n. Duping.]

defs: To deceive; to trick; to mislead by imposing on one's credulity; to gull; as, dupe one by flattery.
quotes: Ne'er have I duped him with base counterfeits.
